clusters.title: Russian strikes kill several in Ukraine's Dnipro
clusters.neutral_headline: Russian strikes kill people in Ukraine

items.title: Russian strikes kill several in Ukraine's Dnipro
items.standfirst: Russian strikes across Ukraine killed at least six people, officials said Saturday, most of them in an attack on a residential building in the eastern city of Dnipro.
items.content: Russian strikes across Ukraine killed at least six people, officials said Saturday, most of them in an attack on a residential building in the eastern city of Dnipro.

items.title: Russian airstrikes kill at least seven people in Ukraine overnight
items.standfirst: Dnipro bore the brunt of the attacks but Odesa and Kharkiv were also targeted in largest onslaught for several daysRussian drone and missile strikes across Ukraine killed at least seven people overnight, including five in the city of Dnipro, Ukrainian local authorities have said.Reports say that at least 34 people have been injured in the strikes, which lasted “practically all night”, according to the Dnipropetrovsk regional head, Oleksandr Hanzha. The bodies of four people were found in the ruins of a house destroyed in the attacks, and workers continued to search for bodies on Saturday morning. Continue reading...
items.content: Dnipro bore the brunt of the attacks but Odesa and Kharkiv were also targeted in largest onslaught for several daysRussian drone and missile strikes across Ukraine killed at least seven people overnight, including five in the city of Dnipro, Ukrainian local authorities have said.Reports say that at least 34 people have been injured in the strikes, which lasted “practically all night”, according to the Dnipropetrovsk regional head, Oleksandr Hanzha. The bodies of four people were found in the ruins of a house destroyed in the attacks, and workers continued to search for bodies on Saturday morning. Continue reading...

items.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
items.standfirst: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.
items.content: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

ai_summary: Russian strikes kill at least six people in Ukraine
Most deaths occur in Dnipro city
Attacks target residential building and other areas
At least 34 people are injured in the strikes
Bodies of four people found in ruined house

clusters.title: Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
clusters.neutral_headline: Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell

items.title: Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: The Justice Department ended its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's renovation project, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro announced Friday.
items.content: The Justice Department ended its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's renovation project, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro announced Friday.

items.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.
items.content: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

items.title: US justice department drops criminal investigation against Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: Move will remove obstacle for confirmation of Kevin Warsh, Trump’s pick to replace Powell as Federal Reserve chairSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Department of Justice is dropping its criminal investigation against the Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, clearing the path for Donald Trump’s new nominee for chair to be confirmed.Jeanine Pirro, Trump’s appointed US attorney for the District of Columbia, said in a social media post that she had directed her office to close its investigation into renovations at the Fed headquarters that went over budget. Continue reading...
items.content: Move will remove obstacle for confirmation of Kevin Warsh, Trump’s pick to replace Powell as Federal Reserve chairSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Department of Justice is dropping its criminal investigation against the Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, clearing the path for Donald Trump’s new nominee for chair to be confirmed.Jeanine Pirro, Trump’s appointed US attorney for the District of Columbia, said in a social media post that she had directed her office to close its investigation into renovations at the Fed headquarters that went over budget. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Justice Department ends criminal investigation into Jerome Powell
Investigation related to Fed building renovation project
President Trump accused Powell of improper cost overruns
US Attorney Jeanine Pirro announces end of investigation
Move clears path for new Federal Reserve chair nominee

clusters.title: Social media influencer dies six days after car hit her outside London nightclub
clusters.neutral_headline: Social media influencer dies after car accident

items.title: Social media influencer dies six days after car hit her outside London nightclub
items.standfirst: Klaudia Zakrzewska, 32, was hit at about 4.30am in Soho in incident that left 58-year-man with life-changing injuriesA social media influencer has died six days after a car hit her and other pedestrians outside a nightclub in London, police said.Klaudia Zakrzewska, 32, was injured in Argyll Street, Soho, at about 4.30am on 19 April and was pronounced dead on Saturday. Continue reading...
items.content: Klaudia Zakrzewska, 32, was hit at about 4.30am in Soho in incident that left 58-year-man with life-changing injuriesA social media influencer has died six days after a car hit her and other pedestrians outside a nightclub in London, police said.Klaudia Zakrzewska, 32, was injured in Argyll Street, Soho, at about 4.30am on 19 April and was pronounced dead on Saturday. Continue reading...

items.title: Influencer dies days after being hit by car outside Soho nightclub
items.standfirst: Klaudia Zakrzewska, 32, from Essex, dies in hospital after a collision on Argyll Street in Soho.
items.content: Klaudia Zakrzewska, 32, from Essex, dies in hospital after a collision on Argyll Street in Soho.

clusters.title: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty on same day U.S. approves firing squads
clusters.neutral_headline: Pope opposes death penalty as US approves firing squads

items.title: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty on same day U.S. approves firing squads
items.standfirst: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.
items.content: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.(Image credit: Christopher Furlong)

items.title: Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
items.standfirst: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.
items.content: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)

items.title: US to allow firing squads, gas, and electrocution for federal executions
items.standfirst: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.
items.content: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

ai_summary: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty
US Justice Department allows firing squads for executions
Move aims to ramp up and expedite capital punishment
Pope says death penalty is inadmissible
US to also allow gas and electrocution for executions

clusters.title: Woman arrested after two children die in Wolverhampton house fire
clusters.neutral_headline: Woman arrested after children die in house fire

items.title: Woman arrested after two children die in Wolverhampton house fire
items.standfirst: Officers continue with inquiries after West Midlands police confirm deaths of boys aged one and three A woman has been arrested after a house fire in Wolverhampton on Friday in which a one-year-old and three-year-old boy died, West Midlands police have said.Emergency services were called to the property in the south of the city at about 8.30pm on Friday, with first responders attending from West Midlands police fire and ambulance services. Continue reading...
items.content: Officers continue with inquiries after West Midlands police confirm deaths of boys aged one and three A woman has been arrested after a house fire in Wolverhampton on Friday in which a one-year-old and three-year-old boy died, West Midlands police have said.Emergency services were called to the property in the south of the city at about 8.30pm on Friday, with first responders attending from West Midlands police fire and ambulance services. Continue reading...

items.title: Woman arrested after two children die in Wolverhampton house fire
items.standfirst: Police said two boys, aged one and three, died at the scene. They confirmed a woman was in custody.
items.content: Police said two boys, aged one and three, died at the scene. They confirmed a woman was in custody.

ai_summary: Two children die in house fire in Wolverhampton
Woman arrested in connection with the incident

clusters.title: Low voter turnout in Gaza as West Bank holds local elections
clusters.neutral_headline: Low voter turnout in Gaza local elections

items.title: Low voter turnout in Gaza as West Bank holds local elections
items.standfirst: Palestinians in the West Bank and central Gaza voted Saturday in municipal elections, the first since the war in Gaza erupted, with low turnout and a limited political field. Nearly 1.5 million people are registered to vote in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, as well as 70,000 people in Gaza's Deir el-Balah area, according to the Ramallah-based Central Elections Commission.
items.content: Palestinians in the West Bank and central Gaza voted Saturday in municipal elections, the first since the war in Gaza erupted, with low turnout and a limited political field. Nearly 1.5 million people are registered to vote in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, as well as 70,000 people in Gaza's Deir el-Balah area, according to the Ramallah-based Central Elections Commission.

items.title: Palestinians cast their ballots as Gaza holds first election in 21 years
items.standfirst: Palestinians are voting in Gaza’s first municipal elections in 21 years. Around 70,000 people are registered to vote.
items.content: Palestinians are voting in Gaza’s first municipal elections in 21 years. Around 70,000 people are registered to vote.

items.title: Gaza area votes in first municipal election in 20 years
items.standfirst: Gaza officials say Deir el-Balah vote is a 'pilot', while occupied West Bank also goes to first polls since 2023 war.
items.content: Gaza officials say Deir el-Balah vote is a 'pilot', while occupied West Bank also goes to first polls since 2023 war.

ai_summary: Palestinians vote in municipal elections
Low turnout reported in Gaza and West Bank
Around 70,000 people registered to vote in Gaza
Elections are first in 21 years in Gaza
Voting takes place in Deir el-Balah area

clusters.title: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain PM responds to reported US punishment plans

items.title: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
items.standfirst: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday refused to be drawn into a dispute with the United States over reports that the Pentagon is weighing whether to punish members of NATO that fail to support American operations in the Iran war. Among those in the firing line is Spain, which has refused to allow US forces involved in the war to use bases on its territory or airspace. Spain says that US-Israeli actions in the Iran war contravenes international law.
items.content: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday refused to be drawn into a dispute with the United States over reports that the Pentagon is weighing whether to punish members of NATO that fail to support American operations in the Iran war. Among those in the firing line is Spain, which has refused to allow US forces involved in the war to use bases on its territory or airspace. Spain says that US-Israeli actions in the Iran war contravenes international law.

items.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
items.standfirst: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.
items.content: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

ai_summary: Spanish PM comments on reported US plans
US reportedly considers punishing NATO members
Spain refuses to support US operations in Iran war
NATO says no provision to expel members
Spain says US actions contravene international law
